items.title: Putin’s invasion comes home as Ukraine expands attacks inside Russia
items.summary: With Russia still showing no signs of ending its invasion, Ukraine is expanding a strategic bombing campaign that aims to undermine the logistics supporting the Kremlin war machine, writes David Kirichenko. items.title: ODIHR’s latest report adds to the growing evidence of Russia’s contemptible conduct in Ukraine: UK statement to the OSCE
items.summary: The UK condemned Russia’s ongoing aerial attacks on Ukraine and cited ODIHR’s latest report which shows evidence of civilian harm, arbitrary detention and efforts to erase Ukrainian identity in the temporarily occupied territories.

items.title: Colombia’s path to growth and opportunity through cooperation with the United States
items.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella will take office as Colombia's next president on August 7, inheriting a challenging fiscal deficit, elevated borrowing costs, and softening investor confidence. As his government works to restore growth, the Atlantic Council's US–Colombia Strategic Alignment Coalition outlines how strengthened trade and investment with the United States can boost economic opportunity across agribusiness, energy, critical minerals, and tourism. The post Colombia’s path to growth and opportunity through cooperation with the United States appeared first on Atlantic Council .

items.title: The Nordic-Baltic Eight policy brief: The circles of security
items.summary: Russia’s war against Ukraine has profoundly worsened Europe’s security environment, but it has also spurred deeper regional cooperation. The challenge now is to ensure that this growing number of initiatives remains purposeful, adds concrete value, and strengthens both the EU and NATO.

items.summary: The EU is considering a full maritime services ban on Russian oil. While the final proposal is still under negotiation, the measure would replace the current oil price-cap regime by prohibiting European and allied companies from providing maritime services for Russian oil exports, regardless of the sale price.
